title: Market Sizing — Method, Not Number
subtitle: Two TAM/SAM/SOM funnels — Phase 1/2 software market and Phase 3 hardware market — and the inputs we are missing for each
description: This is deliberately NOT a set of concentric circles with dollar figures. A defensible market size needs operator counts, size distributions, and willingness-to-pay data we do not yet have. Because AquaVision is now two markets layered on top of each other — a broad Phase 1/2 SaaS/farm-management software market, and a narrower Phase 3 hardware-monitoring market nested inside a subset of it — this chart shows TWO funnels side by side, each marked with the specific input required to fill it. A founder who shows the method and names the missing input for BOTH markets is more credible than one who presents a fabricated number for either.

series.name: Phase 1/2 — Software/SaaS market definition funnel (farm-management app, addressable by anyone with a smartphone and a pond)

data.stage: TAM — all aquaculture operators globally (any scale, any system)
data.required_input: Global smallholder + commercial aquaculture operator counts, not production volume
data.note: Do NOT use the USD 312.8bn industry output as TAM — that is the value of the FISH, not the addressable software market. See industry-statistics.json#/how_to_use_these_statistics.

data.stage: SAM — reachable geography, smartphone/data access, target language and species
data.required_input: Philippine + SEA smartphone-penetration-adjusted operator counts; species and language coverage of the initial app
data.note: Much larger than the Phase 3 SAM below because there is no capital-equipment threshold — a smallholder with a phone qualifies.

data.stage: SOM — realistic near-term free-tier signups and paid conversions
data.required_input: Organic/community-led acquisition rate, activation rate, free-to-paid conversion rate — see kpi-definitions.json#/phase_1_2_saas_kpis, all currently awaiting_data
data.note: Constrained by acquisition and conversion funnel performance, not by a capital or regulatory barrier — this is the fundamental difference from the Phase 3 funnel below.

series.name: Phase 3 — Hardware/monitoring market definition funnel (OmniDrone + IoT + PondTwin, commercial/enterprise)

data.stage: TAM — all aquaculture operations above the sensor-affordability threshold
data.required_input: Global operator counts by system type and scale
data.note: This is the original hardware-era TAM stage, unchanged, now explicitly scoped to Phase 3 rather than to the whole company.

data.stage: SAM — reachable geography, suitable systems, above scale threshold
data.required_input: Philippine + SEA operator counts; the buoy-crossover scale threshold
data.note: Excludes RAS, deep offshore, and single small ponds below the threshold where a buoy wins. See market-sizing-tam-sam-som.json#/market_definition. A SUBSET of the Phase 1/2 SAM above, not a separate population — the Phase 3 customer is a Phase 1/2 user who has outgrown manual tracking.

data.required_input: Dock network capacity, service capacity, regulatory permission, adoption rate
data.note: Constrained by dock coverage and BVLOS, not by demand.

the_blocking_inputs.note: Render these prominently — the missing inputs ARE the content of this chart, for both funnels.

inputs.input: Number of aquaculture operations in the Philippines by system and scale
inputs.status: NOT OBTAINED — the single most important missing datum in the repository; blocks both funnels

inputs.input: Smartphone/data penetration among Philippine smallholder aquaculture operators
inputs.status: NOT OBTAINED — blocks the Phase 1/2 SAM specifically

inputs.input: Organic acquisition, activation, and free-to-paid conversion rates
inputs.source: Live app usage once Phase 1 launches
inputs.status: NOT MEASURED — blocks the Phase 1/2 SOM; cannot exist before launch

inputs.input: Cage counts per lake (Taal, Laguna de Bay, etc.)
inputs.status: NOT OBTAINED — blocks the Phase 3 SAM
NOT COMPUTED — sets the Phase 3 SAM

inputs.input: Measured willingness to pay (subscription price and hardware-service price)
inputs.source: Paid pilots for Phase 3; conversion data for Phase 2

rendering_guidance: Render both funnels with the value cells EMPTY and the required-input labels filled. The honesty of the empty cells is the point. If a viewer asks 'where are the numbers', the answer — 'we need the BFAR operator counts and here is exactly why we will not guess them, for both the software and hardware markets' — is stronger than a fabricated figure. Render the two funnels side by side so the nesting relationship (Phase 3 SAM is a subset of Phase 1/2 SAM) is visually obvious.
